title: Acceptance and Constraints
desc: A practical look at how identifying your coordinates allows you to build solutions.
body:
One of the biggest lessons I learned growing up with Friedrich's ataxia is that acceptance is the foundation of strength.

When my coordination began changing, I spent time fighting the reality of it. Designing workarounds begins with seeing our coordinates clearly.

"Identifying constraints is the first step of construction. It is finding the clearest path on your coordinates."

In digital design, constraints are normal. We deal with code limits and screen boundaries. We design responsive layouts to fit the exact parameters available. When you treat physical or personal limitations as simple parameters, you focus your energy on what you can build today.

title: The Skydiving Tape Method
desc: How a roll of tape and a jump from 14,000 feet taught me the reality of daily adaptation.
body:
August 27, 2021. I was scheduled to jump out of a airplane. After monsoon rains delayed us three times, the skies cleared and we got the green light to fly.

Just before boarding, my tandem expert realized a problem: 'We need you to lift and hold your legs up for landing so they don't break. Since you can't control them, we have a problem.'

He stayed calm and focused on a workaround. He ran to the supply cabinet and returned with a roll of white duck-tape. He taped my legs together so I could lift them as a single unit using my upper body strength. It worked perfectly.

"When life gives you coordination blocks, find the nearest roll of duck-tape."

That jump taught me that adaptation is gritty, immediate, and practical. When you want to build a solution, you work with the available parameters to design a bridge.

title: How to Build a Voice Companion Avatar
desc: A step-by-step breakdown on how I trained ChelloAI on my personal guidelines and voice parameters to help write for me.
body:
Typing is a physical struggle. As coordination changes, pushing keys is exhausting. That's why I use AI models as my typing assistants.

ChelloAI was born out of a simple need: I needed a digital helper that could communicate my thoughts in my voice. To build it, I compiled a text corpus detailing my life timeline, my design experiences, and specific conversational parameters.

The rules I used to train the companion focus on keeping the writing authentic, avoiding marketing buzzwords, and framing challenges positively without seeking pity. By loading this custom data, I now have an assistant that speaks with my direct attitude, acting as a conversational partner.

title: The W.I.N. Reframe Matrix
desc: A practical reflection tool to map out physical, strategic, or budget constraints and decide what outcome to build next.
body:
Whether you are a business owner dealing with a tight budget or an individual dealing with a health coordinate, we all face boundaries. I created the W.I.N. framework to audit these limits and find direction.

The matrix is split into three phases:

  1. Warrior Story: Write down the limitations you are facing. Acknowledge them fully as parameters.
  2. Inspiring Impact: Identify the highest value outcome you can produce within these coordinates.
  3. Nurturing Outcomes: Break down the daily steps. Use AI tools to handle repetitive tasks and save your energy.

By moving through this matrix, you focus your energy on what you can create from where you are today.

title: Designing Around the Heat: Momentum When Energy Runs Low
desc: How to keep your midyear momentum going by treating physical limitations as simple design parameters.
body:
Summer in late June is in full swing. The midday heat is rising, and with Friedrich's ataxia, that heat means my physical energy drains twice as fast. My coordination takes a hit, my muscles tighten, and my body demands that I slow down.

But resting my legs does not mean stopping my momentum. This midyear stretch is all about staying consistent when the elements fight against your progress.

Instead of fighting the fatigue, I treat my physical limits as simple design parameters. If my hands are too stiff to type my thoughts today, I shift my approach. I rely on AI-assisted voice transcription to build my funnels and write my copy. The technology meets me exactly where my physical energy pauses, translating my voice into clean, structured digital assets.

God built us with an incredible capacity to adapt. When one path closes, He opens a creative detour. We just have to be willing to look for the tools that light up the new way.

"Limitations do not define your capacity; they simply redirect your strategy."

My challenge for you today is simple: Look at your biggest bottleneck right now. Stop trying to smash through it with sheer brute force. Instead, design a path around it. Switch your tools, change your medium, or ask for help.

Keep moving forward. The summer energy is ours to claim, and we are just getting started.

Much love, party people! That was awesome, the next one will only be better!

title: Designing Through the Heat
desc: How treating our daily limitations as design parameters keeps our midyear momentum alive.
body:
June is winding down, and the summer heat is officially here. We are sitting right at the halfway mark of the year. It is easy for the momentum we started with in January to fizzle out under the blazing sun. Today, I sat at my desk looking at my projects, feeling the physical weight of Friedrich's ataxia demanding my attention.

In the past, I would have fought this physical fatigue, trying to force my way through. But years of building tech systems and living with this condition have taught me a better way. I do not see my physical limits as a dead end anymore. Instead, I treat them as design parameters. Just like building a clean marketing funnel or writing code, constraints show you where to simplify.

When you accept your constraints, you stop wasting energy on frustration and start directing it toward creative solutions.

For me, that means relying heavily on voice-to-text AI tools to capture ideas when my hands are tired, and keeping my daily task list down to three non-negotiables. God did not call us to burn ourselves out. He called us to stewardship. Using technology to honor my body's pace is how I stay consistent.

Your challenge today: Look at your biggest goal for the second half of the year. What is your biggest constraint right now? Stop fighting it. Build a system, find a tool, or adjust your path to design around it. You do not need perfect conditions to make progress.

Keep moving forward, one small step at a time.

Much love, party people! That was awesome, the next one will only be better!

title: The Beauty of the Boundary
desc: How treating our toughest limitations as simple design parameters helps us build faster, smarter, and with more faith.
body:
Here we are at the tail end of June. The heat is rising, and we are officially at the halfway mark of 2026. It is the perfect moment to stop, breathe, and look at the scoreboard. Not to judge ourselves, but to adjust our strategy.

Lately, my body has been reminding me of its strict boundaries. Friedrich's ataxia does not take summer vacations. Some days, my coordination is a chaotic puzzle. But instead of viewing these physical boundaries as walls, I choose to see them as design parameters. When a programmer builds an app, they do not cry about memory limits; they write tighter, more elegant code to work within them. That is how I view my life and my business.

Constraints are not stop signs; they are blueprints for creative workarounds.

When my hands are too heavy to type out a marketing funnel, I do not stall. I plug in my voice-to-text tools, let the AI draft the structure, and use my voice to shape the message. The constraint actually forced me to find a faster, more sustainable way to build.

My challenge for you today is simple: What is your biggest bottleneck right now? Stop trying to smash through it with brute force. Instead, accept it as a design parameter. How can you build a beautiful path around it?

God did not design us to be limited by our circumstances, but to find glory in how we navigate them.

Much love, party people! That was awesome, the next one will only be better!

title: Faith-Driven Entrepreneurship: Building Business with Higher Purpose
desc: How aligning your daily business goals with faith and service keeps you grounded through physical limitations.
body:
Building Beyond Yourself with Faith-Driven Entrepreneurship

Starting a business when your body fights you every step of the way requires a unique fuel. I find that fuel in my faith. As someone living with Friedrich's ataxia, physical energy fluctuates wildly. Some days my coordination is completely off, and my speech is slurred. Yet, the work goes on. This isn't about raw willpower; it is about anchoring my work in a higher purpose.

When we build projects, shape ideas, or seek services that align with our calling, our daily grind transforms from a struggle into a mission.

The Shift From Control to Trust

In business, we often try to control every outcome. But physical limits force us to surrender that control. I realized that my business is not mine alone—it is a co-creation. If you are struggling to find direction today, pause and ask yourself what you are building for.

Today's challenge: Write down one goal that serves others more than your ego. Aligning your strategy with service is the core of true growth. You can read more about navigating these challenges in the Limitations to Liberation book to see how boundaries can shape a stronger vision.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is faith-driven entrepreneurship?

It is the practice of running a business with spiritual values as the core foundation, prioritizing impact and trust over sheer control.

How do you manage Friedrich's ataxia while running a business?

I rely on smart tools, clear design parameters, and a team-first mindset to keep moving forward when my physical strength is limited.

Much love, party people! That was awesome, the next one will only be better!
